content:
In Part 1, Daniel joined the company as a DevOps engineer and spent his first morning waiting for access.
Instead of giving him permissions one system at a time, you connected his access to his team.
His GitHub team gave him the repositories Platform Engineering uses. In Azure, his Platform Engineering group received the cloud permissions the team needed, limited to the part of the dev environment they actually manage.
If you haven’t read Part 1, you can read it here. You don’t need it to follow this article, though. Here is where we left Daniel.
Two weeks into the job, Daniel opened his first Terraform pull request. The senior engineer reviewed it. Daniel clicked Merge.
GitHub refused.
Daniel sent you a message.
“I thought I had write access.”
He does.
Write access does not remove the rules on main
Daniel has write access to the Terraform repository through the Platform Engineering…
